summary: Updated GitHub Actions workflows so Docker/GHCR image names are normalized to lowercase before build, test, metadata extraction, and push steps. The PR was motivated by a failing build in a fork with uppercase repository/owner text, where package image names derived directly from github.repository or PR repo names could violate registry naming expectations.
what_changed: In build-melee.yml, IMAGE is now computed by concatenating the selected repository name with IMAGE_SUFFIX and piping the full string through `tr '[:upper:]' '[:lower:]'` before writing to GITHUB_ENV.
In publish-pages.yml, the same lowercase normalization was added for the computed IMAGE value used by the pages workflow.

In publish-packages.yml, the previous static `IMAGE_NAME: ${{ github.repository }}/${{ matrix.target }}` environment value was removed and replaced with an explicit `Get image name` step that lowercases `REPO_NAME/IMAGE_SUFFIX` into `IMAGE`.
All Docker build, run, docker/metadata-action, and docker push references in publish-packages.yml were updated from IMAGE_NAME to the normalized IMAGE variable.
The lowercasing fix was applied in both Unix-like shell workflow sections and Windows/PowerShell-adjacent package build sections where image variables are consumed.
smart_moves: Lowercased the complete image path after concatenating repository and suffix, avoiding partial normalization where only the repository or only the target name would be fixed.
Used a simple POSIX `tr '[:upper:]' '[:lower:]'` transformation in workflow shell steps rather than introducing external dependencies.
Centralized the corrected image name in GITHUB_ENV as `IMAGE`, so later workflow steps consistently consume the same normalized value.
Preserved existing workflow behavior for matrix targets, test builds, metadata labels/tags, and pushes while only changing the image naming path.
Applied the same pattern across build, package publishing, and pages publishing workflows, reducing the chance that fork builds would pass in one workflow but fail in another.
decomp_lessons: CI image names derived from GitHub repository names should be normalized before use because fork owners or repository names may contain uppercase letters even if the main repository does not.
For decomp projects that rely on reproducible containerized build environments, workflow portability across forks matters; CI should not assume canonical lowercase organization or repository names.
When changing a workflow variable name such as IMAGE_NAME to IMAGE, update every downstream build, run, metadata, and push reference to avoid inconsistent image tags.
Small infrastructure fixes can unblock contributors whose forks have naming differences without changing source matching or build logic.
naming_conventions: Docker/GHCR package image names should be lowercase.
The workflow uses `IMAGE_SUFFIX` for matrix target-specific suffixes and stores the final normalized image path in `IMAGE`.
Avoid using raw `${{ github.repository }}` directly as a package image name unless it is normalized first.
The PR replaced `IMAGE_NAME` with `IMAGE` for the final computed image reference.
review_feedback: No review comments were present in the provided PR context.
The PR body explicitly cites a failing build in @DRGN-DRC's fork as the motivation for the fix.
follow_up_queries: Find other workflows deriving Docker image names from github.repository without lowercasing.
Search for remaining IMAGE_NAME references in GitHub Actions workflows.
Audit package publishing workflows for fork-safe repository name handling.
Check whether other registry names or artifact names need lowercase normalization.
